name: Random.General

unions.name: Config
unions.comment:
A `Config` describes how the internal random number generator works.
see `makeConfig` for more.

values.name: andMap
values.type: Generator s a -> Generator s (a -> b) -> Generator s b

values.name: andThen
values.type: (a -> Generator s b) -> Generator s a -> Generator s b

values.name: bool
values.type: Config s -> Generator s Bool

values.name: choice
values.type: Config s -> a -> a -> Generator s a

values.name: choices
values.type: Config s -> List (Generator s a) -> Generator s a

values.name: constant
values.type: a -> Generator s a

values.name: filter
values.type: (a -> Bool) -> Generator s a -> Generator s a

values.name: float
values.type: Config s -> Float -> Float -> Generator s Float

values.name: frequency
values.type: Config s -> List ( Float, Generator s a ) -> Generator s a

values.name: generator
values.comment:
This allows you to create base generators such as the `int` or `float` generator.
Only use this if you really know what you are doing.
In almost all cases it is better to create generators that are based on some other generators
using the combinations functions provided in this library.

If you really need this, a generator is just a function of type `s -> (a, s)`.
E.g. a function that takes the current state of your seed, gets some value out of it and generates a new seed.
values.type: (s -> ( a, s )) -> Generator s a

values.name: int
values.type: Config s -> Int -> Int -> Generator s Int

values.name: list
values.type: Int -> Generator s a -> Generator s (List a)

values.name: makeConfig
values.comment:
`makeConfig` takes two arguments, a `next` function and a `peel` function.
The `next` function describes how to generate a new internal state from the current internal state.
The `peel` function describes how to map the internal state to a single integer.

As an example, let's see how to implement a terrible RNG:

    incrementRNG =
        makeConfig
            (\s ->
                -- Bitwise.shiftRightZfBy 0 is used to truncate the state to 32 bits
                s + 1 |> Bitwise.shiftRightZfBy 0
            )
            identity
values.type: (s -> s) -> (s -> Int) -> Config s

values.name: map
values.type: (a -> b) -> Generator s a -> Generator s b

values.name: map2
values.type: (a -> b -> c) -> Generator s a -> Generator s b -> Generator s c

values.name: map3
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d) -> Generator s a -> Generator s b -> Generator s c -> Generator s d

values.name: map4
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e) -> Generator s a -> Generator s b -> Generator s c -> Generator s d -> Generator s e

values.name: map5
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> f) -> Generator s a -> Generator s b -> Generator s c -> Generator s d -> Generator s e -> Generator s f

values.name: maxInt
values.type: Int

values.name: maybe
values.type: Generator s Bool -> Generator s a -> Generator s (Maybe a)

values.name: minInt
values.type: Int

values.name: oneIn
values.type: Config s -> Int -> Generator s Bool

values.name: pair
values.type: Generator s a -> Generator s b -> Generator s ( a, b )

values.name: sample
values.type: Config s -> List a -> Generator s (Maybe a)

values.name: step
values.type: Generator s a -> s -> ( a, s )
